description: ACDC Methods Library with generic formula grammar and structured output taxonomy

_design_notes.formula_grammar: Analysis methods use Wilkinson-Rogers notation with role-tagged placeholders. Derivations use assignment notation. See formula_grammar section for the BNF.
_design_notes.output_taxonomy: Outputs are organized into output_classes, each with an indexing dimension and a statistics vector. This replaces the flat list of output roles from v0.2.
_design_notes.cardinality_markers: In generic_expression: <role>+ = one or more, <role>* = zero or more, <role>? = zero or one, <role> = exactly one
_design_notes.unified_schema: Both derivation and analysis methods share the same top-level structure. Derivations use formula.notation='assignment' and have a single output_class of type 'computed_value'. Analyses use formula.notation='wilkinson_rogers' or 'survival' and have richer output_classes.

formula_grammar.description: BNF grammar for method expressions. Analysis methods use Wilkinson-Rogers style; derivations use assignment style.
bnf: (* A*B expands to A + B + A:B *)

statistics_vocabulary._description: Reusable statistic definitions referenced by output_classes across methods

output_class_templates._description: Reusable output class patterns. Methods reference these by template_ref and can override fields.

type3_tests.description: Type III (or other SS type) omnibus tests, one per model term

parameter_estimates.description: Solution for fixed effects, one row per model coefficient

ls_means.description: Least-squares means (adjusted marginal means) per factor level or level combination

contrasts.description: Pairwise or custom contrasts between LS means

test_result.description: Single overall test result (for non-regression methods)

association_measures.description: Effect size / association measures with inference

survival_table.description: Life table with survival estimates at each event time

event_summary.description: Summary counts of events and censoring per group

computed_value.description: Single derived/computed output value (for derivation methods)

multivariate_tests.description: Multivariate test statistics for overall hypothesis

methods.name: Subtraction
methods.description: Computes the difference between two numeric values

output_classes.description: Result: minuend - subtrahend

methods.name: Division
methods.description: Computes the quotient of two numeric values

output_classes.description: Result: numerator / denominator

methods.name: Percent Change
methods.description: Computes proportional change as a percentage

output_classes.description: Result: 100 * (post - pre) / pre

methods.name: Date Difference
methods.description: Computes the difference between two dates in specified units

output_classes.description: The difference in the specified unit

methods.name: Aggregation
methods.description: Aggregates values within a partition using specified function

output_classes.description: The aggregated value per partition group

methods.name: Categorization
methods.description: Maps continuous or discrete values to categories

formula.default_expression: result := CASE WHEN conditions THEN categories END
formula.generic_expression: result := CASE WHEN <conditions> THEN <categories> ELSE <default_category> END

output_classes.description: The category label

methods.name: Threshold Comparison
methods.description: Compares value against threshold and returns result

output_classes.description: Comparison result flag

methods.name: Apply Imputation
methods.description: Replaces missing values with a pre-computed imputed value. This is the application step only — the imputed_value must be computed upstream by one of the M.ImputedValue_* methods. Designed for use in transformation pipelines.
methods._design_note: This method has no imputation_method configuration. The strategy is determined by which M.ImputedValue_* method is upstream in the transformation pipeline. This makes each step independently testable and auditable.

input_roles.name: imputed_value
input_roles.description: Pre-computed replacement value (from an M.ImputedValue_* method)

output_classes.description: The resulting value (original if non-missing, otherwise imputed)

methods.name: Last Observation Carried Forward
methods.description: Computes the last non-missing observed value within a partition, ordered by a time variable. The result carries forward the most recent non-missing value and is intended as input to M.Imputation.

formula.default_expression: imputed_value := LOCF(value) OVER (partition ORDER BY time_order)
formula.notes: LOCF scans backwards in time_order within each partition and returns the most recent non-missing value. If no prior non-missing value exists, the result is missing.

assumptions: Time ordering within partition is unambiguous
At least one prior non-missing value exists for carry-forward to produce a result
Missing data pattern may be monotone or intermittent

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: Source value to carry forward (may contain missing values)

input_roles.name: partition
input_roles.description: Grouping variables defining the carry-forward scope (typically subject, parameter)

input_roles.name: time_order
input_roles.description: Variable defining the temporal ordering within partition (e.g., AVISITN, ADY)

output_classes.description: The last non-missing value carried forward

methods.name: Baseline Observation Carried Forward
methods.description: Uses the baseline value as the imputed value for all missing post-baseline observations within a partition. Conservative strategy assuming return to baseline.

formula.notes: The baseline value is propagated to all rows within the partition. This is a lookup, not a carry-forward — the imputed value is the same regardless of when the observation is missing.

assumptions: A non-missing baseline value exists for each partition
Return-to-baseline is a clinically reasonable assumption for the endpoint

input_roles.name: baseline_value
input_roles.description: The baseline value for the subject/parameter (typically BASE or value at ABLFL='Y')

input_roles.name: partition
input_roles.description: Grouping variables defining scope (typically subject, parameter)

output_classes.description: The baseline value to use as imputation

methods.name: Worst Observation Carried Forward
methods.description: Uses the worst (most extreme) observed value within a partition as the imputed value. Worst is defined by the direction configuration: 'high' takes the maximum, 'low' takes the minimum.

formula.notes: WORST is an alias for MAX when direction='high' (e.g., adverse events, lab toxicity) or MIN when direction='low' (e.g., efficacy scores where lower is worse). Only non-missing observed values up to the current time are considered.

configurations.name: direction
configurations.description: Which direction is 'worst': 'high' = MAX is worst (e.g., toxicity), 'low' = MIN is worst (e.g., efficacy where higher is better)

configurations.name: scope
configurations.description: Whether to consider only values observed up to the current time point (cumulative) or all observed values in the partition (all_observed)

assumptions: At least one non-missing observed value exists in the partition
The direction of 'worst' is clinically defined for the endpoint

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: Source values from which the worst is selected

output_classes.description: The worst observed value within the partition

methods.name: Mean Imputation
methods.description: Computes the arithmetic mean of all non-missing observed values within a group as the imputed value. Common for baseline covariates or when a group-level central tendency is an appropriate replacement.

formula.notes: The partition defines the scope of the mean computation. For subject-level mean imputation, partition by subject. For group-level, partition by treatment group. The mean excludes the missing values being imputed.

assumptions: At least one non-missing value exists in the group
Missing data are Missing Completely at Random (MCAR) or Missing at Random (MAR) for unbiased mean estimation
Mean imputation underestimates variance — not suitable when variance estimation is important

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: Source values from which the mean is computed

input_roles.name: group_partition
input_roles.description: Grouping variables defining the mean computation scope (e.g., treatment group, site, or subject)

output_classes.description: The group mean of non-missing observed values

methods.name: Median Imputation
methods.description: Computes the median of all non-missing observed values within a group as the imputed value. More robust to outliers than mean imputation.

formula.notes: Same partitioning logic as mean imputation. Preferred when the distribution is skewed or when outliers may distort the mean.

assumptions: At least one non-missing value exists in the group
Missing data are MCAR or MAR
Median imputation underestimates variance — not suitable when distributional shape must be preserved

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: Source values from which the median is computed

input_roles.name: group_partition
input_roles.description: Grouping variables defining the median computation scope

output_classes.description: The group median of non-missing observed values

methods.name: Zero Imputation
methods.description: Uses a fixed constant (default 0) as the imputed value for all missing observations. Simplest strategy; appropriate when missingness can be interpreted as absence of the measured quantity (e.g., zero cost, zero count).

formula.notes: No data-dependent computation. The constant is always used regardless of observed values.

configurations.name: constant
configurations.description: The fixed value to use for imputation. Defaults to 0 but can be set to any value (e.g., 999 for sentinel, or a clinically meaningful default).

assumptions: Zero (or the specified constant) is a meaningful replacement for the missing value in the clinical context

output_classes.description: The constant imputation value

methods.name: Window Lookup
methods.description: Retrieves value from another row within partition

formula.default_expression: result := LAG(value, 1) OVER (partition ORDER BY order_by)

configurations.name: order_by
configurations.description: Variable defining row order within partition

output_classes.description: The looked-up value

methods.name: Rounding
methods.description: Rounds numeric value to specified precision

output_classes.description: The rounded value

methods.name: One-sample t-test
methods.description: Tests whether the mean of a single sample differs from a hypothesized value

formula.notes: Intercept-only model; the test is against the hypothesized mu

output_classes.description: Overall one-sample t-test result
Estimated mean with inference

assumptions: Normality of data (or large sample size)

methods.name: Paired t-test
methods.description: Tests whether the mean difference between paired observations differs from zero

formula.notes: Intercept-only model on the within-subject differences

output_classes.description: Paired t-test result
Mean of paired differences with inference

input_roles.name: response
input_roles.description: The paired difference values (or the raw values at two timepoints)

methods.name: Two-sample t-test
methods.description: Tests whether means of two independent samples differ significantly

formula.notes: Single factor with exactly two levels

output_classes.description: Two-sample t-test result
Group means
Difference between group means

methods.name: ANOVA
methods.description: Analysis of Variance - compares means across multiple groups

formula.notes: One or more factors; interactions can be added with ':' or '*' operator

output_classes.description: ANOVA table: one row per model term
Marginal means per factor level
Pairwise differences between group means

methods.name: ANCOVA
methods.description: Analysis of Covariance - ANOVA with continuous covariate adjustment

formula.notes: Covariates adjust the group comparison. Adding covariate:group interaction tests homogeneity of regression slopes. Multiple covariates and factors are supported.

output_classes.description: Model fit measures
Type III tests for each model term (covariate, group, interactions)
Solution for fixed effects (intercept, covariate slopes, group effects)
Least-squares means per group level, adjusted for covariates
Pairwise LS mean differences between group levels
Residual variance estimate

assumptions: Homogeneity of regression slopes (if no covariate:group interaction)
Linear relationship between covariate and response

input_roles.name: covariate
input_roles.description: Continuous covariate(s) for adjustment (e.g., baseline value, age)

methods.name: Mixed Model for Repeated Measures
methods.description: Linear mixed model for longitudinal data with structured R-side covariance. No random effects (G-side); within-subject correlation modeled through the residual covariance structure.

formula.default_expression: response ~ covariate + time + group + time:group | subject / time
formula.notes: The part after '|' specifies the repeated measures structure: subject is the clustering unit, time is the repeated factor within subject. Multiple covariates and fixed effects are supported. The time:group interaction is typically the term of primary interest for treatment comparison at each visit.

output_classes.description: Model fit measures for covariance structure selection
Type III F-tests for each fixed effect term
Solution for all fixed effect coefficients
LS means by group, by time, or by group×time. The indexing depends on which LSMEANS statement is requested.

sub_types.name: ls_means_by_group_time
sub_types.description: LS means for each group at each timepoint

sub_types.name: ls_means_by_group
sub_types.description: Marginal LS means averaged over time

sub_types.name: ls_means_by_time
sub_types.description: Marginal LS means averaged over group

output_classes.description: Treatment comparisons. Typically the primary analysis: group difference at each timepoint (from time:group interaction).

sub_types.name: group_contrast_at_time
sub_types.description: Group difference at a specific timepoint (e.g., Treatment vs Placebo at Week 24)

sub_types.name: time_contrast_within_group
sub_types.description: Change over time within a group (e.g., Week 24 vs Week 4 within Treatment)

sub_types.name: overall_group_contrast
sub_types.description: Overall group difference marginalized over time

output_classes.description: Estimated R-side covariance matrix parameters

input_roles.name: response
input_roles.description: The response variable at each timepoint (e.g., change from baseline)

input_roles.name: covariate
input_roles.description: Continuous covariate(s) (e.g., baseline value, age, weight)

input_roles.name: time
input_roles.description: Timepoint/visit variable defining the repeated dimension

input_roles.name: group
input_roles.description: Treatment group (and optionally additional factors like region, sex)

input_roles.name: subject
input_roles.description: Subject identifier for the repeated measures clustering

methods.name: Chi-squared Test
methods.description: Tests association between two categorical variables using Pearson's chi-squared statistic

formula.notes: Both response and group are categorical. For RxC tables (more than 2x2).

output_classes.description: Chi-squared test of independence

methods.name: Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel Test
methods.description: Stratified analysis for 2x2xK tables controlling for strata. Tests the null hypothesis of no association between response and group after adjusting for strata.

formula.notes: The '|' denotes stratification. Multiple strata variables produce cross-classified strata.

output_classes.description: CMH test of conditional independence
Common (Mantel-Haenszel) odds ratio across strata
Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of odds ratios across strata

assumptions: Homogeneous odds ratios across strata (for common OR interpretation)

methods.name: Fisher's Exact Test
methods.description: Exact test of independence for 2x2 contingency tables. Preferred over chi-squared when expected cell counts are small.

formula.notes: Both variables must be binary for the standard 2x2 Fisher test.

output_classes.description: Fisher's exact test result
Conditional maximum likelihood odds ratio

methods.name: Logistic Regression
methods.description: Models binary outcome as function of predictors via a link function (logit by default)

formula.notes: Generalized linear model with binomial family. Multiple covariates and factors supported. For stratified analyses, can include strata as a fixed effect.

output_classes.description: Model fit measures
Type III likelihood ratio or Wald tests per term
Maximum likelihood estimates on log-odds scale
Odds ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

assumptions: Linear relationship between log-odds and predictors

input_roles.name: group
input_roles.description: Treatment group and/or other categorical factors

methods.name: Kaplan-Meier Estimator
methods.description: Non-parametric survival function estimation with optional grouping

formula.notes: If no group is specified, estimates a single survival curve. With a group variable, produces stratified curves.

output_classes.description: Summary of events and censoring per group
Life table with survival estimates at each event time
Median survival time per group with confidence interval
Survival probability at specific landmark times

input_roles.name: group
input_roles.description: Optional grouping variable for stratified curves

methods.name: Log-rank Test
methods.description: Compares survival distributions between groups using a rank-based test

formula.notes: Multiple group factors can be included. Strata variables (after adjusting) produce stratified log-rank tests.

output_classes.description: Log-rank (or weighted) test result

methods.name: Cox Proportional Hazards
methods.description: Semi-parametric regression for survival data modeling the hazard function

formula.notes: Multiple covariates, factors, and interactions supported. The strata() function creates a stratified baseline hazard (one per stratum level) without estimating a coefficient for that variable.

configurations.name: ties
configurations.description: Method for handling tied event times

output_classes.description: Model fit and global tests
Overall model significance tests (likelihood ratio, Wald, score)
Type III Wald tests per model term
Regression coefficients on log-hazard scale with hazard ratios
Hazard ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated coefficients)

input_roles.name: group
input_roles.description: Treatment group and/or other categorical factors

input_roles.name: strata
input_roles.description: Stratification variable(s) for stratified Cox model

methods.name: Multivariate Analysis of Variance
methods.description: Tests the hypothesis of no overall treatment effect across multiple correlated response variables simultaneously

formula.notes: Left-hand side uses cbind() to specify the multivariate response. In long format with a parameter identifier, the model is fit as response ~ group with subject and parameter defining the multivariate structure.

output_classes.description: Multivariate omnibus test statistics
Univariate Type III tests per response variable (follow-up to multivariate test)
Regression coefficients per response variable
